metadata.title: Lere Local Government
metadata.description: Official website of Lere Local Government Area, Kaduna State, Nigeria.

slides.title: Lere – A Land of Heritage and Agriculture
slides.subtitle: Lere Local Government Area is known for its agricultural strength and historic landmarks in Kaduna State.

slides.title: Lere – Communities Supporting Development
slides.subtitle: Farmers and traders across Lere contribute significantly to food production and commerce in Kaduna State.

paragraphs: Lere Local Government Area is located in the northern region of Kaduna State, Nigeria. The administrative headquarters is Saminaka town.
The LGA is home to several ethnic groups including Hausa, Fulani, and other indigenous communities that have lived in the region for generations.
Agriculture is the primary economic activity, with crops such as maize, millet, sorghum, beans, and groundnuts cultivated across the area.

chairman.name: Executive Chairman, Lere Local Government
messageParagraphs: Welcome to the official website of Lere Local Government Area. Our administration is committed to strengthening agriculture, improving infrastructure, and enhancing public services for our communities.
Lere is blessed with hardworking farmers, vibrant markets, and a rich cultural heritage that contributes to Kaduna State's development.
Through collaboration with our communities and stakeholders, we will continue to pursue sustainable development across the LGA.

cards.title: Maize Farming
cards.subtitle: Staple Crop
cards.description: Maize is widely cultivated across Lere communities and serves as a staple crop for households.

cards.title: Millet & Sorghum
cards.subtitle: Grain Production
cards.description: Millet and sorghum are major grains grown by farmers throughout the LGA.

cards.title: Livestock Rearing
cards.subtitle: Animal Husbandry
cards.description: Cattle, goats, sheep, and poultry are raised across rural communities within Lere.

news.subtitle: Stay informed about development projects, community programs, and government initiatives in Lere.

hero.title: About Lere
hero.subtitle: Discover the culture, geography, and economy of Lere Local Government Area.

location: Neighboring LGAs: Kubau, Kauru, Ikara, and Sanga.
demographics: Major Ethnic Groups: Hausa, Fulani, and other indigenous groups.
economy: Main Economic Activities: Farming, livestock rearing, and trading.
Climate: Tropical savannah climate suitable for agriculture.

geographyEconomy.title: Geography & Economy
geographyEconomy.location: Lere lies in northern Kaduna State and is known for its fertile farmland and historic landmarks such as Lere Rock.
geographyEconomy.climate: The area experiences a rainy season between May and October and a dry season from November to April.
mainActivities: Crop Farming: Maize, millet, sorghum, beans, and groundnuts.
Livestock Rearing: Cattle, goats, sheep, and poultry.
Local Markets: Agricultural trading and commerce across communities.

hero.title: Government
hero.subtitle: Leadership and administrative structure of Lere Local Government.

structure.intro: Lere Local Government operates through an executive leadership supported by a legislative council and administrative departments.
executive: Supervisory Councilors responsible for major sectors.
legislative: The Legislative Council consists of elected councilors representing wards in Lere.
A Speaker elected among the councilors presides over legislative sessions.

hero.title: Development Projects
hero.subtitle: Infrastructure and community development initiatives across Lere Local Government Area.

projectsPage.introParagraph: Lere Local Government continues to invest in roads, schools, healthcare, and agricultural support to improve the quality of life for residents.

hero.title: Agriculture in Lere
hero.subtitle: Agriculture remains the backbone of Lere's economy.

agriculturePage.overview: Farmers across Lere cultivate maize, millet, sorghum, beans, and groundnuts while raising livestock to support food production and local commerce.
